examples: One future possibility is that improved monitoring can help predict where and when bleaching will occur, which might potentially enable us to mitigate its effects.
It doesn't really mitigate against the confinement he has perpetrated on Lolita.
The leases come with painstaking stipulations to mitigate any possible environmental harm to species like the polar bear.
And the relaxed attitude of the Lao people in general should help mitigate development greed.
The latest ads revolved around the claim that milk can mitigate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.
